ipo.headline: India's first AI IPO: Fractal Analytics announces dates for Rs 2,834 crore public issue
ipo.summary: Fractal Analytics announced dates for its Rs 2,834 crore IPO, billed as India’s first pure-play AI listing. The issue includes fresh shares and an offer-for-sale, funding global expansion, AI products and acquisitions, amid improving profitability and rising investor interest globally.

ipo.headline: Bharat Coking Coal Q3 Results: Revenue drops 25% YoY; Coal India arm slips into losses to Rs 23 crore
ipo.summary: Bharat Coking Coal posted a Q3FY26 net loss as revenue fell sharply year-on-year. Sequential improvement, lower expenses and post-IPO interest offered some relief, though shares slipped on weak earnings despite broader market strength after strong listing and heavy investor demand.

nifty.headline: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Wednesday
nifty.summary: Nifty saw profit-taking after a gap-up opening but held above key Fibonacci and moving average supports. Analysts flagged a shift to buy-on-dips, cooling volatility and improving momentum, even as global markets weakened and select heavyweights influenced overall market sentiment.

nifty.headline: Stock markets cheer India-U.S. trade deal: Sensex, Nifty surge 2.5%
nifty.summary: The trade deal also propelled a sharp rally in textile, leather, gems and jewellery, seafood exports and specialty chemicals stocks

nifty.headline: Market Highlights: Sensex settles 2,073 pts higher on trade deal euphoria, Nifty above 25,700; Adani Ports rises 9%, Bajaj Fin 7%
REITs and InvITs outperform traditional benchmarks indices in 2025: ICRA Analytics
nifty.summary: India’s listed REITs and InvITs surged in 2025, outperforming Nifty50 and G-Secs. REITs led on leasing-driven yields, power InvITs stayed resilient, while road InvITs lagged. Strong cash flows and rate dynamics reinforced trusts as preferred yield investments for investors nationwide.

nifty.headline: The deal that changed Nifty playbook: India-US trade pact throws up over 70 winning stock ideas
nifty.summary: The Indian stock market surged, nearing all-time highs following the announcement of a significant India-US trade deal. This agreement, which includes tariff reductions on Indian imports, is expected to boost exports, accelerate corporate earnings, and strengthen the rupee, signaling the start of a potential economic boom.

nifty.headline: Stock Market LIVE Updates: Sensex Holds 2,300-Point Rally, Nifty Tests 25,800 After India-US Trade Deal; Financials Lead
nifty.summary: The BSE Sensex rose 2,319 points. It had risen as much as 4,200 points to 85,871.The NSE Nifty 50 rose 2.8% to 25,783.95 after rising as much as 5% intraday.

nifty.headline: Gems and jewellery stocks sparkle on India-U.S. trade deal
nifty.summary: In the equity market, the BSE benchmark traded 2,390.71 points or 2.94% higher at 84,065.17, and the NSE Nifty quoted 707 points or 2.8% up at 25,794.30

nifty.headline: Ashish Kacholia says bear market in smallcaps may have ended after India-US trade deal
nifty.summary: Veteran investor Ashish Kacholia sees India’s smallcap bear market ending after the India-US trade deal. Following tariff cuts and clarity on trade ties, Indian equities, the rupee, and bonds rallied sharply, with the Nifty surging up to 5%, highlighting renewed investor confidence and potential inflows into smallcap and midcap stocks.

nifty.headline: Baap of all trade deals! What Modi-Trump announcement means for your stocks
nifty.summary: A landmark India-US trade deal has sent the Sensex and Nifty soaring. This agreement is expected to bring back foreign institutional investors, reversing significant outflows. Several sectors, including auto ancillaries, chemicals, textiles, and IT services, are poised to benefit. The deal enhances earnings visibility and strengthens India's position as a safe haven.

nifty.headline: Rupee climbs sharply, rallies 1% to 90.40 vs dollar in early trade
nifty.summary: India's financial markets surged Tuesday following a trade deal that slashed U.S. tariffs on Indian goods. The Nifty 50 index jumped nearly 5%, and the rupee climbed over 1% against the dollar. This development is expected to alleviate investor concerns and attract foreign capital back into Indian assets.

nifty.headline: Rs 13 lakh crore boom! Sensex surges 3,500 pts, Nifty soars nearly 5%. India-US trade deal among top factors behind rally
nifty.summary: Indian benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex surged significantly following a long-awaited trade deal between India and the US. The agreement, which includes reduced reciprocal tariffs, has eased concerns that had impacted the market. A strengthening rupee further supported domestic assets, boosting investor sentiment and market capitalization.

nifty.headline: Sensex Today | Nifty50 | Stock Market Live Updates: Nifty hits fresh all-time high in pre-open trade
Positive breakout: These 11 stocks close cross above their 200 DMA
nifty.summary: ​As of February 2, 2026, 19 Nifty500 stocks closed above their 200-day moving average. Eleven top performers gained more than 3%, signalling strong uptrends. Stocks such as Mahindra & Mahindra, Polycab India, and Petronet LNG are showing positive momentum, highlighting potential buying opportunities for traders following technical indicators and market trends.​

nifty.headline: GIFT Nifty Hints At Gap-Up Open, Trades Above 25,900 After US-India Trade Deal
nifty.summary: The GIFT Nifty—an early indicator of Nifty 50's performance—traded at 25,919.50 as of 6:30 am, suggesting a gap-up open after India and the United States agreed to a trade deal that cuts reciprocal tariffs on Indian imports to 18%.

nifty.headline: Stock markets’ over 1% bounceback reflects clarity on tax
nifty.summary: Indian stock markets bounced back on Monday. The Nifty and Sensex recovered from budget day losses. This rebound followed a global sell-off in commodities and precious metals. Short covering and easing concerns over a securities transaction tax hike supported the market. Auto sales and falling crude oil prices also boosted sentiment. Foreign investors sold shares, while domestic investors bought them.

nifty.headline: Trade Setup For Feb 3: Nifty Finds Resistance At 25,400-25,500 Levels As Volatility Remains Heightened
nifty.summary: Key support will be near the panic low of Budget session at 24,500-24,600 levels.

While the policy shift boosts manufacturing prospects, he cautions that earnings upgrades won’t be automatic and maintains that large-caps remain a safer strategic bet than small-cap exporters.

trade.headline: Manufacturing stuck at 14% for years; P Chidambaram slams Budget 2026, questions Modi govt's economic strategy

Key concerns include India's manufacturing sector remaining stagnant, a persistent unemployment crisis, and a slow pace of fiscal consolidation. Chidambaram also pointed out a significant cut in capital expenditure and a widening trade deficit with China.

These issues occurred during a period of heightened market activity, preventing traders from capitalizing on a sharp rally in Indian equities following an India-US trade deal. Zerodha acknowledged the problems and stated its teams were investigating.

general.summary: Minister of State for Civil Aviation Murlidhar Mohol told the Rajya Sabha that all aircraft accidents and serious incidents reported in the country in the last one year have been reviewed by the AAIB.

general.summary: The country's aviation sector is deregulated and airlines are free to operate flights and deploy capacity on routes based on their commercial viability and market considerations.

general.headline: First tranche of research innovation fund to be spent by March
general.summary: Although allotted ₹20,000 crore for the Financial Year 2025-26, the Department of Science and Tehnology has not been able to spend any of that corpus until January

general.headline: Moltbook Security Breach: Social Network For AI Bots 'Exposed' Human DMs, Credentials
general.summary: The breach exposed private direct messages exchanged between AI agents, email addresses of more than 6,000 human owners, and over one million login credentials.

general.headline: Apple Hits Record 28% Value Share In India As Smartphone Market Leans Towards Premium Devices
general.summary: India's smartphone market grew 1% year-on-year (YoY) in volume and 8% YoY in value in 2025.

general.headline: FanCode Wins Exclusive TV, Digital Rights For ISL 2025-26
general.summary: With this acquisition, FanCode will deliver a digital-first viewing experience for ISL fans.

general.headline: Trump Shares India Gate's Photo, Plans Washington's Triumphal Arch: 'Ours Will Be Greatest Of All'
general.summary: Trump's post comes amid advanced plans to construct a new triumphal arch in Washington DC to mark the 250th anniversary of American independence.

general.headline: Fed chair nominee Warsh may want smaller Fed holdings, but that's not easy to do
general.summary: Kevin Warsh, tapped to become the ‍next Federal Reserve chair, may want to significantly contract the central bank's multi-trillion-dollar ​balance sheet, but experts agree that financial realities strongly indicate accomplishing this goal will be difficult and slow, if it can be done at ⁠all.

general.headline: Discussions are ongoing on ‘how to deal with’ regulation on investments from China: DEA Secretary
general.summary: Economic Affairs Secretary Anuradha Thakur said that while the Ministry of Finance was not discussing the issue, talks were on in other Departments of the government.

general.headline: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Tuesday
general.summary: Markets rebounded after volatility triggered by the STT hike and higher borrowing plans, aided by policy continuity and falling crude prices. Analysts warn sentiment remains cautious below the 200-day average, advising selling rallies despite medium-term confidence amid global tensions ongoing.

general.headline: Union Budget 2026: Not so poll-driven, but signals focus on global headwinds
general.summary: While the Budget did include announcements touching poll-bound States, the measures were packaged within broader multi-State initiatives that reflected the Government’s attempt to balance domestic political expectations with global economic uncertainties
